description: Five tests of whether the TEP dust signal persists when using proxies for potential depth that are partially independent of stellar mass. Each test computes partial correlations controlling for M* and z.

test2_ssfr_residual.description: sSFR residual after removing mass+z trend → dust
test2_ssfr_residual.interpretation: If sSFR residual (mass-independent) predicts dust, this supports an effect beyond mass scaling.

test3_chi2_proxy.description: SED chi² as TEP distortion proxy → dust
test3_chi2_proxy.interpretation: Higher chi² indicates poorer SED fit. Under TEP, high-Gamma_t galaxies have distorted SEDs. If chi² predicts dust at fixed M* and z, it supports the isochrony-bias interpretation.

test4_photoz_uncertainty.description: Photo-z uncertainty (z_84-z_16) as SED-shape proxy → dust
test4_photoz_uncertainty.interpretation: Photo-z uncertainty depends on SED shape, not mass directly. If it predicts dust at fixed M* and z, this is a mass-independent signal consistent with TEP-distorted SEDs.

test5_age_residual.interpretation: If the mass-independent component of age ratio predicts dust, this supports a time-dependent mechanism (TEP) rather than a pure mass proxy.

summary.assessment: 5/5 indirect proxies significantly predict dust at fixed M* and z in the full_z4_10 sample; 2/5 also track Γt directly; 0/5 satisfy both conditions with a consistent depth ordering.

test2_ssfr_residual.description: sSFR residual after removing mass+z trend → dust
test2_ssfr_residual.interpretation: If sSFR residual (mass-independent) predicts dust, this supports an effect beyond mass scaling.

test3_chi2_proxy.description: SED chi² as TEP distortion proxy → dust
test3_chi2_proxy.interpretation: Higher chi² indicates poorer SED fit. Under TEP, high-Gamma_t galaxies have distorted SEDs. If chi² predicts dust at fixed M* and z, it supports the isochrony-bias interpretation.

test4_photoz_uncertainty.description: Photo-z uncertainty (z_84-z_16) as SED-shape proxy → dust
test4_photoz_uncertainty.interpretation: Photo-z uncertainty depends on SED shape, not mass directly. If it predicts dust at fixed M* and z, this is a mass-independent signal consistent with TEP-distorted SEDs.

test5_age_residual.interpretation: If the mass-independent component of age ratio predicts dust, this supports a time-dependent mechanism (TEP) rather than a pure mass proxy.

summary.assessment: 4/5 indirect proxies significantly predict dust at fixed M* and z in the z_gt_8 sample; 1/5 also track Γt directly; 1/5 satisfy both conditions with a consistent depth ordering.

headline.conclusion: At z>8, 4/5 indirect proxies associate with dust after mass+z control, but only 1/5 also track Γt directly and 1/5 satisfy both criteria with a consistent depth ordering. The proxy suite therefore narrows — but does not close — the mass-proxy degeneracy.
headline.honest_caveat: All five proxies are INDIRECT measures of potential depth. They are partially correlated with mass through astrophysical channels unrelated to TEP. A definitive test requires a mass-independent dynamical measurement of potential depth, such as NIRSpec IFU velocity dispersions for z>8 galaxies. This test is proposed as a Cycle 4 JWST program.
